name: Mark.MarkAttributes
summary: The MarkAttributes class defines the mark attributes.

constructors.name: Mark.MarkAttributes
constructors.summary: Creates a new default mark attributes instance that loads standard attributes.

params.name: modelObject
params.doc: The ModelObject defining the content to load.

constructors.name: Mark.MarkAttributes
constructors.summary: Creates a new mark attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

params.name: modelObject
params.doc: The ModelObject defining the content to load.

methods.name: IsEqual
methods.summary: Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

params.name: ObjectToCompare
params.doc: The object to be used for comparing.

returns.doc: True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

methods.name: LoadAttributes
methods.summary: Loads attributes from the attribute file.

params.name: attributeFilename
params.doc: The filename of the attribute file.

properties.name: Angle
properties.summary: The actual angle of the mark, in degrees. Can differ from the rotation angle set by the user for marks, e.g. 45-degree column marks in GA drawings or when placing mark along part edges.

properties.name: ArrowHead
properties.summary: The arrowhead of the leader line(s).

properties.name: Content
properties.summary: The mark content as a list of elements.

properties.name: CustomPresentation
properties.summary: Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type

properties.name: Frame
properties.summary: The frame attributes of the mark's labels.

properties.name: PlacingAttributes
properties.summary: The placing attributes that the object should use. Using these you can specify whether the object is automatically arranged in the drawing or not.

properties.name: PreferredPlacing
properties.summary: The type of placing the object should use. See the preferred placing types for different types of placing.

properties.name: RotationAngle
properties.summary: The mark rotation angle set by the user. Unit is degrees.
